# Transcrição: 03-Repeat Purchases

- **Fonte:** 03-Repeat Purchases
- **Duração:** 05:16
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:04]

Now the third way of Generating growth for your business going beyond more customers and getting those customers to spend more with you Is to get customers to buy from you again? Now there are many ways to achieve that so as part of the for funnel system We focus heavily on this is something which I see Facebook ad accounts missing out is if you have existing customers Try and resell to them. This is a typical funnel view that we set up for our existing customer Campaigns I call it the reverse sales funnel because essentially what you're doing is Taking the full funnel system and flipping it on its head So if you think about the cold warm hot Process that we go through on the full funnel system the reverse happens with existing customers Because at the point of purchase they are still hot But as they as time goes by and they don't repurchase again They become warm and then cold So generally this is the type of funnel that I have set up both in my ad account and on email To try and get people to buy again now that depends on what kind of products or service you've got Might maybe it doesn't make sense for someone to buy within 30 days again, especially for example If it's a product like an LED light for example, which you may not be replacing so soon But it doesn't mean that you can't upsell or cross sell them something else So in the LED light example if they've bought one and they're happy with it And we can put some measures in place to track them then let's get them to buy another one Maybe for themselves for another part of the house or for someone there that would benefit from it as well Also think about pulling people through for reviews and referrals and things like that because if they're happy customers Then you need to monetize them whether you do it directly through Getting them to buy again or giving you value in another way those first 30 days are so important And quite often what we'll do is get a thank you video into that customer journey post purchase Either via email or Facebook ads to show them that we appreciate them But also keep them keep our products of service front of mind Around kind of what could be coming next in terms of future promotions as well and then get them into our email flow to try and get them to reengage and repurchase and this is all part of a system called the RFM model Recency frequency and monetary value and what you're trying to do here is to try and Maximize what you're getting from your existing customers and there's different ways of doing that So there's word of mouth for example If you've got a happy customer get them to tell their friends and family or maybe there's incentives to buy again So, you know, here's your existing customer offer buy again within three days for You know 25% off or whatever because if you've acquired that customer once and you can reacquire them get again through email

## [03:04 - 05:16]

There's no additional cost per acquisition really involved things like loyalty So can you reward them for future purchases? but you maybe you know if it's an consumable for example hair shampoo maybe you know after their third purchase they get one free or something like that something that really hooks them into your products and service and keeps them coming back for more or maybe there's a Subscription element as well. So going back to the hair shampoo example if they bought one product and they're happy get them on subscription find out how frequently they shower give them an idea of How long it might last and get them to buy again Through subscription and then you've got reoccurring revenue as well. So it's really important to Think about the different ways that you can monetize your Existing customer to give you direct value ie revenue or indirect ie reviews referrals and things like that because this will have a big impact on your expansion and growth beyond just looking at Facebook ads as an acquisition Vehicle, you know, some people will be happy making a loss on the first purchase as Hop as happens with subscription businesses So when I'm running ads for subscription generally, we are you know as far as cost per acquisition goes We make a loss on that first purchase But we are working on the back end to make the profit after purchase three four five Etc and that's okay and knowing that and modeling that helps us to scale up our front-end traffic generation With an eye on what a profitability is now was that is Out of scope of what we generated what we created for the BPM method for Facebook has program They are things that as they come up You want to be able to look at and find ideas and opportunities to expand into so traffic expansion and Customer repurchase are massive opportunities once you've got your ad account running and operational To get even more value into your business
